_doc: STQ-02 category (3) — ambiguous circuit (post-ask-resolved). Inspector says 'Circuit six Zs nought point three two.' but the schedule only has circuits 1-4. In production this triggers ask_user('out_of_range_circuit') which blocks the dispatcher until the inspector confirms via tool_result. For DETERMINISM — the golden-divergence harness uses only the write dispatcher (no ask registry, no ws fan-out), see scripts/stage6-golden-divergence.js runToolCallPath — this fixture captures the POST-RESOLVED state: the inspector has confirmed 'Upstairs sockets', so both paths emit create_circuit(6,'Upstairs sockets') followed by record_reading(6,zs,'0.32'). The ask_user round-trip itself is locked by Phase 3's dispatcher-ask tests; duplicating it here would add a cross-cutting registry dependency that is explicitly out of scope for SC #6.
_deviation_from_production: Live session would see one ADDITIONAL round where ask_user fires and blocks on a tool_result. This fixture elides that to a single create_circuit+record_reading sequence so both paths are SSE-deterministic. The shape under test is the final slot outcome, which is identical either way.
_requirement: STT-11 + STQ-02 category 3 + STS-03 (create_circuit dispatcher) + STS-01 (record_reading dispatcher).

pre_turn_state._doc: Schedule has circuits 1-4 only. Circuit 6 does NOT exist — this is what production would have triggered an ask_user on; in this fixture both paths jump directly to the create+record sequence.

transcript: Circuit six, Zs nought point three two.

sse_events_legacy._comment: Legacy post-resolution — record_extraction emits both a circuit_updates[create(6)] and extracted_readings[(6,zs,0.32)].

sse_events_tool_call._comment: Round 1 — two tool_use blocks: create_circuit(6) then record_reading(6,zs). Dispatcher requires the circuit to exist on the snapshot before record_reading runs; stage6-dispatchers-circuit.js dispatchCreateCircuit mutates session.stateSnapshot.circuits[circuit_ref] synchronously, so the second dispatcher sees the new circuit in the same round.
